hero.subheadline: $29.6 million. 34,000 residents. One place to see where the money goes and what the council is deciding right now.

budget.heading: Where your $870 goes
budget.subheading: College Park's FY2026 operating budget is $29.6M — roughly $870/resident/year. Your property tax rate is 33.5¢/$100 — half the PG County average of 63.27¢/$100.
budget.clickHint: Click a category to filter council items below

council.heading: This week at City Hall
council.subheading: Recent and upcoming items from the College Park City Council — in plain English.
council.noItems: No recent council items in this category.

platform.heading: Civic intelligence for every resident
platform.subheading: The dashboard above is just the beginning. Civic Lens is building the full stack of civic transparency tools for College Park.
platform.languageAvailable: Available in English · Español · 中文

verify.heading: Every summary is verifiable
verify.body: When we summarize a city document, we hash it, store it on IPFS, and register the hash on the Polygon blockchain. Anyone can verify that our AI summarized the exact document the city published — not an edited version.

footer.disclaimer: Data sourced from College Park FY26 Budget Ordinance 25-O-04 (adopted May 20, 2025) and official City Council agendas from collegeparkmd.gov. This site is an independent civic project and is not affiliated with the City of College Park.
footer.tagline: Built for transparency · College Park, MD · 2026
